global.title: Open: This is "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," April 19, 2026 - CBS News
global.summary: CBS highlights that the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, while related coverage points to fresh violence in Kyiv and continued volatility around energy and security.
global.whyItMatters: This helps frame prayer and preaching around fear, instability, and the need for peacemaking in a fragile world.

global.title: Humanoid robots outrun humans in Beijing half-marathon - Reuters
global.summary: Reuters features a striking technology milestone from Beijing, where humanoid robots outpaced human runners in a half-marathon setting.
global.whyItMatters: It offers a timely conversation starter on technology, human dignity, vocation, and how churches disciple people in an AI-shaped culture.

global.title: Skydiver rescued after crashing into scoreboard during Virginia Tech football game - CBS News
global.summary: A dramatic stadium accident ended in rescue rather than tragedy, with a skydiver surviving after crashing into a scoreboard during a football game.
global.whyItMatters: Stories like this remind pastors how quickly ordinary moments can turn urgent, and how communities respond in crisis.

canadian.title: Carney says Canada's economic ties with US are a weakness that must be corrected - The Sun Chronicle
canadian.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney says Canada's deep dependence on the U.S. economy has become a vulnerability and is calling for new investment, trade diversification, more clean energy, and lower internal trade barriers.
canadian.whyItMatters: Economic uncertainty affects household stress, generosity, employment, and congregational care across Canada, including BC.

persecutedChurch.title: IDF says image of soldier destroying Jesus statue in Lebanon is real, vows action - The Times of Israel
persecutedChurch.summary: The IDF says a viral image of a soldier smashing a statue of Jesus in southern Lebanon is authentic, has opened an investigation, and says it will help restore the statue.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a fresh development affecting Christian witness and interfaith tensions in the region, and it invites prayer for protection, repentance, and peace.

persecutedChurch.title: Attempted arson at London synagogue is 3rd attack on Jewish site in past week: "Sustained campaign of violence" - CBS News
persecutedChurch.summary: An attempted arson at a North London synagogue marks the third attack on a Jewish site in a week, amid rising concern over a broader campaign of intimidation.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Pastors should note escalating religious hatred beyond Christian communities and lead congregations in solidarity, vigilance, and prayer for neighbors under threat.

leadership.title: Why The Leadership Traits That Win Promotions Fail Employees - Forbes
leadership.summary: Forbes argues that charisma and visibility often get leaders promoted, while teams actually need trust, accountability, sound judgment, and emotional steadiness.
leadership.whyItMatters: A useful lens for church staffing, elder development, and evaluating leaders by fruit and trustworthiness rather than platform presence.

goodNews.title: Good News: Teen Completes Own “Boston Marathon” in Hospital - NBC News
goodNews.summary: A Massachusetts teenager fighting leukemia turned his daily hospital walks into his own version of the Boston Marathon.
goodNews.whyItMatters: This is a strong pastoral illustration of courage, perseverance, and hope in suffering.
